title: Fruit of Knowledge DNA
wikitext:
[]: Fruit of Knowledge: DNA?             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Aug 03, 2009 11:13 pm As the world is formed with spirals, the (S2) engine acquires energy from its shape, which is the same as DNA. -- CI. S2... a super solenoid/double helix... Fruit of Life. DNA... a super solenoid/double helix... Fruit of Wisdom? Ritsuko describes the Angels as having an 'inherent wave pattern' which resembles human genes when it is arrayed in the form shown on Ritsuko's screen. She does not describe them as having DNA as we know it, the kind that stores itself in a spiral form when not being read or copied. Yes. Although they are composed of a different form of matter, their actual composition, in terms of the arrangement and spacing of the pattern, falls within a 99.89% match of human genes. -- Ritsuko, ep5. Consider that human DNA may have been what awakened Adam: The DNA that was inserted (/dived) into Adam is already physically fused! Fused... A forbidden fusion of the Fruits of Life (S2) and Wisdom (DNA), perhaps? Now consider this: After exploding, Adam lost those fruits. Embryonic Adam definitely lacked a full S2 at the very least, (the theory that it might have been able to grow a new one is a separate issue) and also lost its Chamber of Guf. Likely the DNA too, depending on your personal Kaworu origin theory. So then, perhaps the reason the Angels were seeking to "reset all life" using Lilith is because Adam was no longer usable, whereas Lilith had a Fruit of Wisdom and a Chamber of Guf (and the Angel itself would already have the Fruit of Life, its S2). Now consider this: Perhaps Kaworu, being incarnated into the body of a Lilin, did not have an S2/Fruit of Life, but did have DNA/Fruit of Wisdom. Perhaps Seele told him that an Impact-ready Adam was waiting for him in Terminal Dogma, complete with a Chamber of Guf and an S2. (See the "greatest plothole solved" thread for my take on Kaworu & Seele, which dovetails with this.) But perhaps Kaworu would not have been able to enter Adam all by himself, because he didn't have a Lance of Longinus like Katsuragi's team. That is why he needed Eva-02: a body made from Adam, which, much like clone organ transplant, would not be rejected by the original. He planned to fuse with the Eva, so that he could then enter Adam, and then merge his human body's DNA with the S2 he thought he would find there, causing Third Impact.

[]: Re: Fruit of Wisdom: DNA?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 03, 2009 11:19 pm Monk Ed wrote: Embryonic Adam definitely lacked a full S2 at the very least,

[]: Re: Fruit of Wisdom: DNA?             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Aug 03, 2009 11:44 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Gendo fuses the Adam embryo into his hand for a reason, and if it didn't contain an S2 I can't imagine what that reason is. Plus, GNR gains S2 powers after absorbing it and Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 03, 2009 11:53 pm Hmmmm, most of your arguments are mighty convincing, I have to say. Could it possibly be we're nearing a definitive conclusion to this mess? The only thing I'd continue to argue against is that it seems that Kaworu says he can't fuse with Ni because of its soul but he can control her since the soul has shut itself away. But, other than that, I really can't find any holes in your logic... Too bad AEF isn't around...

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Aug 04, 2009 12:40 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: The only thing I'd continue to argue against is that it seems that Kaworu says he can't fuse with Ni because of its soul but he can control her since the soul has shut itself away.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 2:34 am Fair enough. I think I'm done here. I'd really like to get some other Eva masterminds weighing in on this. It really does seem much of this episode is falling into place.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Aug 04, 2009 3:39 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Fair enough. I think I'm done here. I'd really like to get some other Eva masterminds weighing in on this. It really does seem much of this episode is falling into place.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:12 am Are we saying the Seed of Wisdom is DNA? Loads of things have DNA, but they wouldn't necessarily have wisdom. Perhaps the seed is stored in DNA? Hmm...

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:20 am oOoOoOo wrote: Are we saying the Seed of Wisdom is DNA? Loads of things have DNA, but they wouldn't necessarily have wisdom. Perhaps the seed is stored in DNA? Hmm...

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:25 am I'm starting to see another Judeo-Christian concept misused by Anno just because it seemed "super cool". If the Seed of Wisdom does not grant you a high consciousness, beyond animal intelligence, it really isn't the Seed of Wisdom, is it? I mean, where is the dividing line between naked Adam and Eve and clothed Adam and Eve? It is supposed to be that "wisdom". If even dogs and cats have the Seed of Wisdom, then it fucks up the whole idea of immortality + wisdom = godhood. It just reduces the seeds to identifiers reminding you which of the "moons" you came from. I thought the "Lilim" had the seed, whereas the rest of LCL-based life was merely... LCL-based. Hrrmmmm... confused.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:29 am oOoOoOo wrote: I'm starting to see another Judeo-Christian concept misused by Anno just because it seemed "super cool". If the Seed of Wisdom does not grant you a high consciousness, beyond animal intelligence, it really isn't the Seed of Wisdom, is it? I mean, where is the dividing line between naked Adam and Eve and clothed Adam and Eve? It is supposed to be that "wisdom". If even dogs and cats have the Seed of Wisdom, then it fucks up the whole idea of immortality + wisdom = godhood. It just reduces the seeds to identifiers reminding you which of the "moons" you came from. I thought the "Lilim" had the seed, whereas the rest of LCL-based life was merely... LCL-based. Hrrmmmm... confused.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:34 am oOoOoOo wrote: I'm starting to see another Judeo-Christian concept misused by Anno just because it seemed "super cool". If the Seed of Wisdom does not grant you a high consciousness, beyond animal intelligence, it really isn't the Seed of Wisdom, is it? I mean, where is the dividing line between naked Adam and Eve and clothed Adam and Eve? It is supposed to be that "wisdom". If even dogs and cats have the Seed of Wisdom, then it fucks up the whole idea of immortality + wisdom = godhood. It just reduces the seeds to identifiers reminding you which of the "moons" you came from. I thought the "Lilim" had the seed, whereas the rest of LCL-based life was merely... LCL-based. Hrrmmmm... confused.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:43 am Thank fucking Christ.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:57 am Monk Ed wrote: I wonder what all non-Lilin Lilith-derived biota are considered, then?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Aug 04, 2009 7:02 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: I dunno but you must remember that it seems part of the SoL's duty is to somehow make their planet habitable for their respective life-form. I once noted the very "Evolution VS Creationism" duality in how Lilith and Adam seems to create; with life emerging from Lilith's LCL VS Adam physically reshaping planets so the Angels can live there. It's possible that the necessary life just grew out of Lilith and eventually made the Earth habitable for Lilim or it's also possible that Lilim eventually evolved out of the organisms that were being created. This is one aspect of NGE we'll never be sure of.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 7:12 am Monk Ed wrote: You mean, all other life was basically created for the purpose of making a habitable place for humans? That is so Judeo-Christian.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Tue Aug 04, 2009 7:29 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: It's quite plausible. Since Lilim/humans need a certain blend of organic elements to survive it seems likely that Lilith would have something to do with making sure those things come into being. However, the other possibility is that this happens more naturally via evolution. IOW, organisms emerge from the LCL and Lilim are the ultimate evolution of the long process. I think the latter fits better in the whole Evolution VS Creationist duality between Lilith and Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 7:32 am Hmmm, it's possible. If indeed there are any deeper reasons for her dislike of meat other than being a character trait or a reflection of Anno's feelings then that would likely be it.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Aug 04, 2009 7:49 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Hmmm, it's possible. If indeed there are any deeper reasons for her dislike of meat other than being a character trait or a reflection of Anno's feelings then that would likely be it.

(from this old ANF thread: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=205198#205198[/url] ) Reichu wrote: FYI, the CI basically states that the Super Solenoid has the same shape as DNA.
